step_by_step_reasoning: We identify web and mobile actors routing requests through an API gateway layer to an Elastic Load Balancer (ELB). ELB forwards requests to an EKS cluster hosting microservices, including a continuous payroll engine. This engine interacts bi-directionally with Amazon MSK (Streaming for Kafka) to handle real-time shift events. EKS writes state to Aurora PostgreSQL. Aurora syncs with ElastiCache and Elasticsearch (OpenSearch) for low-latency queries and lookups by the application.

graph.name: ADP Workforce Now Real-Time Payroll Architecture
graph.notes: Scalable human capital management architecture using EKS microservices and real-time Kafka event streaming to calculate up-to-the-minute payroll.

nodes.name: Web Users
nodes.notes: Employees and employers accessing via web browsers.

nodes.name: Mobile Users
nodes.notes: Employees clocking in/out via mobile devices.

nodes.name: API Layer
nodes.notes: Handles incoming API requests from devices.

nodes.name: ELB
nodes.notes: Load balancing cluster distributing traffic to EKS.

nodes.name: EKS Cluster
nodes.notes: Runs hundreds of microservices including continuous calc payroll engine.

nodes.name: Aurora PostgreSQL
nodes.notes: Encrypted system of record with row-level security.

nodes.name: ElastiCache
nodes.notes: Caches user entitlements for fast retrieval.

nodes.name: Elasticsearch
nodes.notes: Powering employee and payroll history searches.

edges.notes: Distributes traffic to microservices in EKS.
EKS publishes clock-out event to Kafka.
Continuous payroll engine reads event to recalculate.
Syncs database data to search cluster.
